alert1: Note: This is not an official Einbürgerungstest. This platform is designed to help you prepare for the Einbürgerungstest.
alert2: This website is a private offering and not an official Federal Office for Migration and Refugees (BAMF) site. The Einbürgerungstest can only be carried out at the BAMF.
hero_subtitle: The free, open-source platform to prepare for Germany's citizenship test. Practice 300+ questions, take mock exams, and track your progress — all without ads.
feature_prepare_desc: Work through 300+ official questions at your own pace. Filter by category, flag tricky ones, and translate into multiple languages.
feature_mock_desc: Simulate the real test with 33 random questions and a 60-minute timer. Build confidence under real exam conditions.
feature_dashboard_desc: Visual charts, daily stats, and exam readiness indicators. Know exactly where you stand and what to focus on.
cta_banner_subtitle: Join thousands of learners preparing for their citizenship test. Free, no ads, no signup required.
faq_a1: "Leben in Deutschland" (Living in Germany) is an official knowledge test administered by the German Federal Office for Migration and Refugees (BAMF). It is designed for people who hold a permanent residence permit and want to demonstrate their knowledge of German society, politics, history, and legal system. The test is part of the integration process and is sometimes required to obtain a permanent settlement permit (Niederlassungserlaubnis).
faq_a2: The Einbürgerungstest (naturalization test) is the official citizenship test for people who want to become German citizens. It tests knowledge of Germany's legal and social system, values, and history. Passing this test is one of the requirements for obtaining German citizenship through naturalization (Einbürgerung). It is administered nationwide by the BAMF.
faq_q3: What is the difference between "Leben in Deutschland" and the Einbürgerungstest?
faq_a3: Both tests use the same pool of 300 general questions plus 10 state-specific questions per federal state. The key differences are: (1) Purpose — "Leben in Deutschland" is for permanent residents demonstrating integration knowledge, while the Einbürgerungstest is specifically for those seeking German citizenship. (2) Pass threshold — for "Leben in Deutschland" you need 15 out of 33 correct answers, for the Einbürgerungstest you need 17 out of 33. (3) Legal effect — passing the Einbürgerungstest fulfills the civic knowledge requirement for naturalization, while "Leben in Deutschland" can count towards integration requirements.
faq_q4: How many questions are on the test?
faq_a4: The test consists of 33 multiple-choice questions. 30 questions are drawn from a general pool of 300 questions covering topics like democracy, history, law, and society. The remaining 3 questions are specific to the federal state (Bundesland) where you take the test.
faq_q5: How many questions do I need to answer correctly to pass?
faq_a5: For the Einbürgerungstest (citizenship test), you need at least 17 out of 33 correct answers to pass. For the "Leben in Deutschland" test, you need at least 15 out of 33 correct answers. Each question has four answer choices, and only one is correct.
faq_q6: How long do I have to complete the test?
faq_a6: You have 60 minutes to complete all 33 questions. Most people finish well within this time. You can work through the questions in any order and revisit previous questions before submitting.
faq_q7: What topics are covered in the test?
faq_a7: The questions cover a wide range of topics organized into categories: Democracy & Politics (parliament, parties, elections), Law & Governance (Basic Law, rights, duties), History & Geography (German and European history, reunification), Economy & Employment (social system, taxes), Education & Religion (school system, religious freedom), Rights & Freedoms (equality, freedom of speech), and state-specific questions about your Bundesland.
faq_a8: Yes, 3 out of 33 questions on the test are specific to the German federal state (Bundesland) where you are registered to take the test. Each state has its own set of 10 state-specific questions, and 3 are randomly selected for your test. These questions cover topics like the state capital, coat of arms, and local government.
faq_q9: Where can I take the test?
faq_a9: The test can only be taken at authorized examination centers (Prüfstellen) designated by the BAMF. These are located throughout all 16 German states, typically at adult education centers (Volkshochschulen) or other certified institutions. You can find your nearest exam center using our Exam Centers directory on this platform.
faq_q10: How much does the test cost?
faq_a10: The test fee is 25 euros. This fee must be paid when you register at your local examination center. The fee is the same regardless of which state you take the test in. If you do not pass, you can retake the test, but you will need to pay the fee again.
faq_q11: Can I retake the test if I fail?
faq_a11: Yes, you can retake the test as many times as needed. There is no limit on the number of attempts. However, you must pay the 25 euro fee each time and register again at an examination center. We recommend using our practice questions and mock exams to prepare thoroughly before each attempt.
faq_a12: This is a free, open-source platform designed to help you prepare for both the "Leben in Deutschland" test and the Einbürgerungstest. It offers all 300+ official practice questions, realistic mock exams with a 60-minute timer, progress tracking with visual charts, multi-language question translations, and an Android app for studying on the go. It is not affiliated with the BAMF — the actual test can only be taken at official examination centers.
faq_a13: Yes, completely free. There are no ads, no paywalls, no subscriptions, and no hidden costs. The platform is open-source, meaning anyone can view and contribute to the code on GitHub. We believe test preparation should be accessible to everyone.
faq_q14: Do I need to create an account?
faq_a14: No. You can start practicing immediately without signing up or creating an account. All your progress is saved locally on your device using your browser's storage. This means your data stays private and is never sent to any server.
center_subtitle: The Einbürgerungstest can only be taken at authorized examination centers (Prüfstellen). Select your state to find test locations near you.
map_section_subtitle: Click on any state to find exam centers or browse state-specific questions.

footer_brand_desc: Free, open-source platform to help you prepare for the German Einbürgerungstest. Practice questions, take mock exams, and track your progress.
feature_no_ads_desc: Focus entirely on learning. No advertisements, no paywalls, no interruptions — just you and your preparation.
feature_multilang_desc: Questions available in German with translations to Hindi, Russian, Turkish, and many more languages to help you understand better.
feature_mobile_desc: Take your preparation on the go with our Android app. Daily reminders, text-to-speech, and offline-ready study tools.
how_it_works_step1_desc: Select your German state to get state-specific questions tailored to your test.
how_it_works_step2_desc: Work through 300+ questions at your own pace with instant feedback and translations.
how_it_works_step3_desc: Simulate real exam conditions with timed 33-question tests and track your pass rate.
how_it_works_step4_desc: Monitor your progress with detailed charts and know exactly when you are exam-ready.
hero_lid_desc: "Leben in Deutschland" (Living in Germany) is the official test for permanent residents. It covers politics, history, and society.
hero_ebt_desc: The "Einbürgerungstest" is for those seeking German citizenship. Same question pool, higher stakes — your path to a German passport.
download_subtitle: Download our free Android app and prepare for the Einbürgerungstest anytime, anywhere — with offline support, daily reminders, and text-to-speech.
download_rating_label: 4.6 out of 5 stars on Google Play
review_1_text: This app is exactly what I needed to prepare for my Einbuergerungstest. The questions are well organized and I love being able to track my progress.
review_2_text: Very useful for practice. The mock exams with the timer helped me manage my time during the real test. Best of all, no annoying ads!
review_3_text: The multi-language translation feature was a lifesaver. Being able to read questions in my native language helped me understand the topics much faster.
eval_section_subtitle: Track when the BAMF evaluates exam results. This data is automatically synced from the BAMF portal.
faq_q15: When will I get my Einbürgerungstest result?
faq_a15: The BAMF typically evaluates exam results within approximately {avgWait} days after the exam date. Currently, the exam from {examDate} is being evaluated (as of {lastSync}). Waiting times may vary depending on the volume of exams.
faq_q16: Which exam date is the BAMF currently evaluating?
faq_a16: The BAMF is currently evaluating results from the exam date {examDate}. This information is automatically synced daily from the BAMF portal (last sync: {lastSync}).
faq_q17: How long does the BAMF take to evaluate the Einbürgerungstest?
faq_a17: Based on the last {historyCount} evaluation cycles, the average waiting time between the exam date and results publication is approximately {avgWait} days. This duration can vary depending on exam volume and time of year.
notification_body: Continue your preparation for the Einbürgerungstest
